title: Kitchen caddy and acessory device and methods for using same
abstract: Disclosed herein are devices and systems for detachably mounting accessories, such as kitchen and bath accessories. In one aspect, a disclosed system encompasses a caddy device for detachably coupling at least one accessory; and at least one accessory configured to detachably couple to the caddy device. The caddy device may be mounted to any surface and used for detachable coupling one or more accessories, such as a kitchen sponge or brush. Also disclosed herein are kits and methods for using the disclosed devices and systems.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed: 1. A detachable accessory system comprising: a caddy device for detachably coupling at least one accessory; and at least one accessory configured to detachably couple to the caddy device. 2. A caddy device for coupling one or more accessories, the device comprising: a caddy device assembly; a mounting mechanism for attaching the caddy device to a mounting surface; and a coupling mechanism configured to attach one or more accessories to the caddy device. 3. The device or system of claim 1 or 2, further comprising an accessory configured to detachably attach to a surface of the caddy device. 4. The device or system of claim 3, wherein the accessory configured to detachably attach to a magnetic mounting surface. 5. The device or system of claim 3, wherein the accessory comprises a kitchen accessory. 6. The device or system of claim 3, wherein the accessory comprises a bathroom accessory. 7. The device or system of claim 3, wherein the accessory comprises a tool. 8. The device or system of claim 3, wherein the accessory comprises an external material that cannot be attached to the caddy device. 9. The device or system of claim 3, wherein the caddy device can be used to hold other tools not inherently able to attach to the caddy device. 10. The device or system of claim 3, wherein the accessory comprises a magnetic sponge or a magnetic brush, or a combination thereof. 11. The device of claim 3, wherein the caddy device comprises a caddy device assembly or caddy device body. 12. The device of claim 3, wherein the caddy device assembly is configured to be vertically mounted or horizontally mounted. 13. The device of claim 3, wherein the device may be made from a plurality of materials such as silicone, plastic, antimicrobial material, film. 14. The device or system of claim 3, wherein the caddy device would have attachment points for the accessory device. 15. The device or system of claim 3, wherein the cady device has attachment properties not present in the surrounding surface. 16. The device or system of claim 3, wherein the caddy device is slender in construction. 17. The device or system of claim 3, wherein the caddy device is flexible in construction. 25 

The device or system of claim 3, wherein the caddy device is made of a material that will not degrade overtime due to exposure. The device of claim 3, wherein the device is flexible The device or system of claim 3, wherein the device is made of a flexible material. The device or system of claim 3, wherein the accessory attachment mechanism does not interfere with the flexibility of the caddy device. The device or system of claim 3, wherein the caddy device is puncture resistant from any accessory attachment mechanism. The device or system of claim 3, further comprising an attachment mechanism on the caddy device. The device or system of claim 23, wherein the attachment mechanism can support the weight of the accessory. The device or system of claim 23, wherein the attachment mechanism is distributed throughout or substantially throughout the entirety of the caddy device. The device or system of claim 23, wherein the attachment mechanism is only located at finite areas of the caddy device. The device or system of claim 23, wherein the attachment mechanism uses a magnetic force. The device or system of claim 23, wherein the attachment mechanism utilizes adhesive means. The device or system of claim 23, wherein the attachment mechanism uses the same means of attaching as the caddy uses to attach to the surrounding surface. The device or system of claim 23, wherein the attachment mechanism is externally exposed on the caddy device. The device or system of claim 23, further comprising an attachment device exposed externally on the device. The device or system of claim 31, wherein the external attachment device does not interfere with the mounting capabilities of the device. The device or system of claim 31, wherein the external attachment device covers the entirety of the caddy device. The device or system of claim 31, wherein the external attachment device is removable. The device or system of claim 34, wherein further comprising concavities for the placement of accessory devices. The device or system of claim 35, wherein the concavities correspond to the attachment mechanism for the accessory. The device or system of claim 35, wherein the concavities allow the accessories to take up less space overall. 

The device or system of claim 35, wherein the concavities allow the accessories to attach more easily to the caddy device. The device or system of claim 38, wherein further comprising a caddy device that can be utilized with a plurality of attachment mechanisms. The device or system of claim 39, wherein a plurality of attachment mechanisms can be paired with the caddy device. The device or system of claim 39, wherein different attachment mechanisms are best suited for specific environments. The device or system of claim 39, wherein further comprising one face of the device having an attachment mechanism. The device or system of claim 39, wherein the face having the attachment mechanism may be an integral part of the device. The device or system of claim 39, wherein the face having the attachment mechanism allows for the replacement of the attachment mechanism used in the device. The device or system of claim 44, further comprising an embedded attachment mechanism. The device or system of claim 45, wherein the embedded attachment mechanism does not hinder the ability for accessories to attach to the device. The device or system of claim 45, wherein the embedded attachment mechanism is shielded from the surrounding environment. The device or system of claim 45, further comprising a mounting mechanism to attach the mechanism to a mounting surface. The device or system of claim 48, wherein the mounting surface may comprise a horizontal surface. The device or system of claim 49, wherein the mounting mechanism can be accomplished using physical means. The device or system of claim 48, wherein the mounting surface may comprise a vertical surface. The device or system of claim 48, wherein the mounting surface may comprise a wall or sink material. The device or system of any preceding claim, wherein the mounting mechanism comprises: a Designer suction cup, Microsuction tape, Geckskin, Removable VHB, Dual lock tape, Magnetic Anchor, Tacky Vacuum Membrane, Thin fdm, Plastic-like thin film, or combinations thereof. The device or system of claim 3, wherein the mounting mechanism is stronger than the attachment mechanisms of the device. The device or system of claim 3, further comprising a horizontal mounting mechanism. 

The device or system of claim 56, wherein the horizontal mounting mechanism prevents the device from sliding along the surface. The device or system of claim 56, wherein the horizontal mounting mechanism relies on bonding rather than high friction to remain attached to the surface. The device or system of claim 56, wherein the horizontal mounting mechanism is integrated into the design of the device. The device or system of claim 56, wherein the bonding strength of the horizontal mounting mechanism can be retained when used vertically. The device or system of claim 56, wherein the mounting mechanism can be reusable. The device or system of claim 56, further comprising a vertical mounting mechanism. The device or system of claim 61, wherein the vertical mounting mechanism can support the weight of any accessories attached to the device. The device or system of claim 61, wherein the vertical mounting mechanism can retain some bonding capabilities in different orientations. The device or system of claim 61, further comprising the ability for the accessory to attach to the mounting surface. The device or system of claim 3, wherein the coupling mechanism of the accessory uses the same mechanism as the attachment mechanism. The device or system of claim 65, wherein the coupling mechanism is magnetic. The device or system of claim 65, wherein the coupling mechanism can repeatedly attach to the coupling surface. The device or system of claim 65, wherein the coupling mechanism is not externally exposed on the accessory device. The device or system of claim 65, further comprising a magnetic coupling mechanism. The device or system of claim 3, wherein the magnetic coupling mechanism is distributed throughout or substantially throughout the accessory device. The device or system of claim 3, wherein the magnetic coupling mechanism is strong enough to support the weight of the accessory through the surrounding body of the accessory. The device or system of claim 3, further comprising an accessory capable of attaching to the device. The device or system of claim 3, wherein the accessory has a component that corresponds to the caddy for coupling. The device or system of claim 3, wherein the device retains its adhesive properties after repeated use. The device or system of claim 3, wherein the accessory device has a specific point to attach to the device. 28 

The device or system of claim 3, wherein the accessory can repeatedly attach to the device. The device or system of claim 3, wherein the accessory can attach to the device using an internal (embedded) mechanism. The device or system of claim 3, wherein the accessory can attach to the device by external means. The device or system of claim 3, wherein the accessory can better attach to the caddy device compared to the surrounding surface. The device or system of claim 3, further comprising a sponge. The device or system of claim 80, wherein the sponge is magnetic. The device or system of claim 80, wherein the sponge has a cavity or pocket for a magnet to be inserted into the sponge. The device or system of claim 82, wherein the sponge uses a different material for the internal lining. The device or system of claim 82, wherein the attachment mechanism used to attach the sponge to the device is removable. The device or system of claim 80, wherein the sponge comprises magnetized material on the outside effective to couple to the caddy device. The device or system of claim 3, further comprising a brush. The device or system of claim 86, wherein the brush is attached so no moment arm of the brush exceeds the attachment strength of the brush to the device. The device or system of claim 86, wherein the brush has an internal removable mechanism to attach to the caddy device. The device or system of claim 86, wherein the brush has a slidable cover for removing the attachment mechanism. The device or system of claim 88, wherein the attachment mechanism is a magnet. The device or system of claim 88, where the brush has a compartment in the handle for holding and dispensing liquid cleaner. The device or system of claim 3, wherein the device may be more sanitary than conventional caddy devices. The device or system of claim 3, wherein the device may promote better airflow than conventional caddy devices. The device or system of claim 91, further comprising a reusable mounting mechanism. The device or system of claim 95, wherein the mounting reusable mounting mechanism does not lose any significant amount of adhesion from prolonged use of the device. The device or system of claim 95, wherein the coupling mechanism is magnetic. 29 

The device or system of claim 95, wherein the attachment system is repeatedly able to attach and detach from the mounting surface. The device or system of claim 95, wherein the device is configured to be compact in design. The device or system of claim 3, further comprising a removable mounting mechanism. The device or system of claim 95, further comprising anti-microbial properties. The device or system of claim, wherein the device comprises copper. A method for detachably attaching an accessory within a sink, the method comprising: a. Mounting a device caddy to a vertical wall of a sink; and b. Detachably coupling a magnetically responsive sink accessory to the device caddy. The method of claim 167, wherein the device caddy is mounted to an outside surface of the sink wall. The method of any 168; wherein the sink accessory is directly attached to an inside surface of the sink wall. 30
